title: 控方证人
summary:
While traveling by train, a man named Raoul Le Verte joins a conversation between Dr. Campbell Clarke, Canon Paffitt, and Sir George Durand regarding the case of Félicité Bauer. Le Verte reveals that he was raised in a home run by a philanthropist named Miss Slade, alongside Félicité Bauer and Annette Lavelle (also referred to as Annette Ravil). He asserts that understanding Annette’s character is essential to understanding Félicité.

Le Verte describes Annette as a passionate and commanding girl who frequently bullied the physically strong but "stupid" Félicité. Although Félicité resented Annette’s mockery and insults regarding her parentage, she continued to follow her. Le Verte recounts an incident where Annette used an early book on hypnosis to manipulate Félicité. By having Félicité stare at a spinning brass ball, Annette successfully commanded her to eat a candle in a playground, claiming it was the best cake she had ever tasted. This prank caused Félicité to experience intense hatred toward Annette, though Annette continued to dominate her.

As they grew older, Annette's ambitions shifted from dancing to singing. Under the guidance of Miss Slade, Annette trained intensely as a singer. Le Verte notes that Miss Slade eventually expressed concern regarding Annette's health, specifically a persistent cough resulting from her excessive practice.
